pi_items.metadata_value: task.json should contain a 'metadata' field with key-value claims to verify
pi_items.verified_value: NO METADATA FIELD EXISTS in task.json
pi_items.source: Direct inspection of benchmarks/cua_world/environments/emoncms_env/tasks/rename_feed/task.json - file only contains: id, version, env_id, description, init, hooks, success

pi_items.verified_value: Emoncms - open-source web application for energy monitoring (emoncms.org, GitHub: github.com/emoncms/emoncms)
pi_items.source: Web search and setup_emoncms.sh script analysis. Confirmed via official Emoncms project at https://emoncms.org/ and GitHub repository https://github.com/emoncms/emoncms

pi_items.verified_value: Feed named 'Appliances' with initial tag='power'. Task requires renaming to 'Appliances Power' with tag='appliances'
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh (lines 14-20) and setup_emoncms.sh (line 282) show feed creation: create_feed('Appliances', 'power', 'W', engine=5, interval=10)

pi_items.verified_value: Data is SYNTHETIC, generated via Python algorithms in setup_emoncms.sh (lines 321-405). 30 days of procedurally-generated time-series data with realistic power curves, solar generation patterns, temperature variations.
pi_items.source: Direct inspection of setup_emoncms.sh seed data generation code. Functions gen_value(), generate_power_curve(), generate_solar(), generate_temp() create synthetic values. Data written directly as PHPFina binary files, not from any external dataset.

pi_items.verified_value: verifier.py is a STUB VERIFIER. Function verify_rename_feed() simply returns {'passed': True, 'score': 100, 'feedback': 'Stub verifier — VLM evaluation is external'} without performing any actual checks.
pi_items.source: Direct inspection of verifier.py (lines 7-9). Comment on line 2-3 states: 'Stub verifier for rename_feed task. Actual verification is done externally via VLM evaluators.'

pi_items.verified_value: Appliances feed created with: name='Appliances', tag='power', unit='W', datatype=1, engine=5 (PHPFina), interval=10 seconds. Historical data: 30 days of synthetic values with realistic patterns.
pi_items.source: setup_emoncms.sh lines 281-282 show: feeds['power2'] = create_feed('Appliances', 'power', 'W', engine=5, interval=10). Seed data generation (lines 321-405) creates 30-day PHPFina binary files.

pi_items.verified_value: Admin user: username='admin', password='admin'. Hardcoded in task_utils.sh (lines 8-9 define EMONCMS_ADMIN_USER='admin', EMONCMS_ADMIN_PASS='admin'). API keys generated via MySQL at setup time.
pi_items.source: setup_emoncms.sh lines 145-148 and task_utils.sh lines 8-9. Admin user created with hardcoded credentials during environment setup.

pi_items.verified_value: Emoncms URL: http://localhost (port 80, default HTTP). Served via Docker container 'emoncms-web' with Apache + PHP.
pi_items.source: setup_emoncms.sh lines 8, 62-65, 170-173, 559. Task description states 'Emoncms at http://localhost'.

pi_items.verified_value: Engine type: 5 (PHPFina). PHPFina is Emoncms' high-precision fixed-interval time-series storage engine for efficient power/energy data logging.
pi_items.source: setup_emoncms.sh line 282: engine=5. setup_emoncms.sh lines 363-404 show PHPFina binary file format implementation (float32 values, .dat and .meta files).

pi_items.verified_value: Feed interval: 10 seconds (matches PHPFina store interval). Data points stored at 10-second intervals, generating 8,640 points per day.

privileged_info_summary: The task.json for rename_feed@1 contains NO 'metadata' field, contradicting the audit instruction premise. The task operates on Emoncms, a real open-source energy monitoring web application. The environment uses synthetic data: 30 days of procedurally-generated time-series values in a PHPFina feed named 'Appliances' (unit: W, interval: 10 seconds, initial tag: 'power'). The task requires renaming this feed to 'Appliances Power' and changing its tag to 'appliances'. The verifier is a stub that unconditionally returns success without validation. Admin credentials are hardcoded (admin/admin). The environment runs Emoncms via Docker with MySQL/Redis backends.
